name: Real Estate Education and Community Housing, INC.
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure counseling, and down payment assistance to low-to-moderate income families in Florida.
description_long:
Real Estate Education And Community Housing, Inc. (R.E.A.C.H.) is a Florida-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in January 2006. The organization operates as a HUD-approved Foreclosure and Housing Counseling Agency and is affiliated with HomeFree-USA. Their stated mission is to inspire and motivate low-to-moderate income community members to achieve homeownership through education, counseling, and grant assistance. R.E.A.C.H. positions itself as a provider of tools and support for financial stability and community strength. The organization serves homebuyers, prospective homeowners, and families facing foreclosure or eviction.

R.E.A.C.H. offers multiple programs aligned with homeownership and financial wellness. Their Homebuyer Education Program uses NeighborWorks curriculum and is approved by the Palm Beach County Commission on Affordable Housing. They conduct 8-hour HUD-approved homebuyer workshops in both virtual and in-person formats, offered in multiple languages. The Foreclosure Counseling and Intervention Programs help families evaluate options and avoid foreclosure in conjunction with government and private agencies. They also provide pre-purchase counseling, money management education, and post-closing home maintenance classes. A Credit Mitigation Program assists with budgeting, savings, and credit issues, with Second Chance Grants available to pay off past-due accounts subject to pre-qualification and funding availability.

What distinguishes R.E.A.C.H. is its multilingual service delivery (workshops offered in native languages when possible), HUD approval status, and integration of multiple support services under one organization. The organization explicitly targets low-to-moderate income populations and provides free educational workshops alongside counseling. Their affiliation with HomeFree-USA and partnership with local mortgage lenders and real estate professionals suggests established community networks. The availability of down payment assistance guidance and grant programs differentiates them from generic credit counseling services.

However, the website lacks specifics on funding caps, geographic service areas, income thresholds for qualification, actual grant amounts available, and wait times for counseling services. The description of the Credit Mitigation Program and Second Chance Grant eligibility criteria are vague. There is no information about counselor credentials, typical counseling timelines, or success metrics. The website does not clearly distinguish between free services and those requiring fees, though a "Fees & Payment" page exists but content is not provided in the sample.
pros: HUD-approved foreclosure and housing counseling agency with established nonprofit status since 2006
Free 8-hour homebuyer education workshops using NeighborWorks curriculum approved by Palm Beach County
Multilingual service delivery with workshops conducted in native languages when possible
Comprehensive program suite including pre-purchase counseling, foreclosure prevention, money management, and post-closing home maintenance classes
Second Chance Grant program available to assist with paying off past-due accounts and credit issues
Down payment assistance guidance and referral support for homebuyer programs
Toll-free access via (855) 48-REACH for easy contact and intake
cons: Website lacks specifics on Second Chance Grant amounts, funding availability, and qualification thresholds
Geographic service area not clearly defined beyond general Florida reference; may be limited to Palm Beach County or specific regions
No information on typical wait times for counseling appointments or counselor credential details
Distinction between free services and fee-based services unclear from homepage; 'Fees & Payment' page content not transparent
No published success rates, client outcomes, or performance metrics to evaluate effectiveness
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Florida from low-to-moderate income households seeking free education and preparation
Homeowners facing foreclosure or eviction seeking counseling and intervention support
Individuals with credit challenges looking for credit mitigation, budgeting assistance, and potential grant support
Non-English speakers seeking homebuyer education in their native language
diagnosis: R.E.A.C.H. is best for low-to-moderate income individuals and families pursuing homeownership or facing foreclosure who need free, nonprofit counseling and education. The main caveat is that while the organization is HUD-approved and established, the website provides limited transparency on grant funding levels, geographic service boundaries, qualification criteria, and actual success outcomes—prospective clients should contact directly at (855) 48-REACH to verify eligibility and service availability.
services: HUD-approved homebuyer education workshops (8-hour virtual and in-person formats)
Money management and financial wellness education
Credit mitigation program with budgeting and credit issue assistance
Second Chance Grants for paying off past-due accounts
Down payment assistance program guidance and referrals
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
